era_context: The Early Dynastic Period III in Mesopotamia (c. 2600–2334 BCE) saw the rise of competing city-states including Lagash, Umma, Uruk, and Ur. Egypt's Fourth Dynasty was in its mature phase under the Old Kingdom. The Indus Valley Civilization was at its urban peak. The traditional Xia Dynasty of China was supposedly in its early centuries, though archaeological confirmation is lacking.
geographic_coverage_gaps: Most of the world outside the Mediterranean, Mesopotamian, Egyptian, and Indus regions
disconfirming_evidence: No events are attested for 2604 BCE. The year is archaeologically and textually indistinguishable from surrounding years.
historiographic_note: 2604 BCE is beyond the temporal resolution of historical documentation. No contemporary sources, inscriptions, or later chronicles record specific events for this year. The historical record for this period is organized by broader chronological units (dynasties, reigns, archaeological phases) rather than individual years. Year-by-year history does not become possible until much later periods with more detailed written records and better chronological anchors.
